name: Emergency CT Head Trauma
description: Emergency CT head for evaluation of acute intracranial trauma, hemorrhage, and fracture

findings.technique: CT of the head was performed without contrast as an emergent study for trauma evaluation.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Epidural Hematoma
sections.content: There is [no epidural hematoma / no hyperdense collection] overlying the brain parenchyma.

sections.name: Subdural Hematoma
sections.content: There is [no subdural hematoma / no hyperdense crescentic collection] along the cerebral convexities.

sections.name: Subarachnoid Hemorrhage
sections.content: There is [no subarachnoid hemorrhage / no hyperdensity in the sulci / no SAH] identified.

sections.name: Intraparenchymal Hemorrhage
sections.content: There is [no intraparenchymal hemorrhage / no intracerebral hemorrhage / no ICH] identified within the brain parenchyma.

sections.name: Intraventricular Hemorrhage
sections.content: There is [no intraventricular hemorrhage / no blood in the ventricles] identified.

sections.name: Contusion
sections.content: There is [no cerebral contusion / no coup injury / no contre-coup injury] identified.

sections.name: Fracture
sections.content: There is [no calvarial fracture / no skull fracture] identified. The temporal bones are [intact].

sections.name: Mass Effect
sections.content: There is [no mass effect / no midline shift / no herniation]. The ventricles are [not compressed / normal].

sections.name: Basal Cisterns
sections.content: The basal cisterns are [patent / open / no compression]. No transtentorial or tonsillar herniation.

sections.name: Soft Tissues
sections.content: The scalp soft tissues demonstrate [no swelling / no hematoma / no laceration].

impression:
1. No acute intracranial hemorrhage, mass effect, or skull fracture.
2. Normal emergent CT head for trauma evaluation.
author: RadReport Templates
